temperature_increases_average_kinetic_en.name: Temperature rise → higher average kinetic energy
temperature_increases_average_kinetic_en.school: When heated, particles of a substance move faster: their average kinetic energy grows proportionally to the absolute temperature (E ~ T).

temperature_increases_effective_collisio.name: Higher average kinetic energy → larger fraction of effective collisions
temperature_increases_effective_collisio.school: At higher average energy a greater fraction of collisions exceeds the activation energy — the reaction speeds up. Hence the Van't Hoff rule: raising the temperature by 10 °C accelerates the reaction 2–4 times.

transition_energy_matches_photon_energy.name: Photon absorption → electron transition to a higher level
transition_energy_matches_photon_energy.school: An electron absorbs a photon whose energy exactly equals the difference between two energy levels and instantly jumps to the upper level (excited state).

exchange_stabilization.name: Exchange stabilisation → lower total energy of the atom
exchange_stabilization.school: Electrons with parallel spins 'meet' in the same region of space less often due to the Pauli exclusion principle. This reduces their electrostatic repulsion — the total energy of the atom decreases. The effect is greatest when the subshell is completely or half filled (all spins parallel).
